dossier_layering.canon_public: Grounded directly in baseline NPC record plus linked polity/institution/locality support where available.
dossier_layering.gm_hidden: Conservative connective tissue derived from agenda, pressure, role logic, and relationship web; not promoted above asserted canon.

core_identity.hidden_identity: None explicitly asserted; hidden layer concerns pressure, leverage, and misread motive rather than a confirmed second identity.
core_identity.ethical_profile: Role-shaped ethics anchored to place, obligation, and consequence rather than free-floating abstraction.
core_identity.general_temperament: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a recruiter still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
core_identity.short_npc_pitch: Sel Stone is a recruiter anchored to Red Hood Guild, where daily institutional pressures make them useful, risky, or both in play.
core_identity.public_role: Sel Stone is a recruiter anchored to Red Hood Guild, where daily institutional pressures make them useful, risky, or bot

setting_placement.location_setting: Elusive independent guild operating in shadowed relation to trade, intrigue, and forbidden or covert work.
who_they_answer_to: the procedural demands of Red Hood Guild
who_depends_on_them: Nora Stone (Both tied to Red Hood Guild.)

public_vs_private.public_face: Known locally through day-to-day work as recruiter. Sel Stone carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.
public_vs_private.common_first_impression: Sel Stone presents as Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a recruiter still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
visible_flaws: can widen small matters through procedure
public_symbols: Sel Stone carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.
public_vs_private.private_truth: Keep Red Hood Guild workable through the obligations of recruiter while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity. Must stay useful at the seam where policy becomes ordinary consequence. Around Red Hood Guild, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of recruiter visibly slip.
public_vs_private.what_they_hide: Must stay useful at the seam where policy becomes ordinary consequence. Around Red Hood Guild, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of recruiter visibly slip.
public_vs_private.what_they_fear_being_known_for: The degree to which their usefulness depends on strain staying barely manageable.
public_vs_private.what_they_are_wrong_about: They may overestimate how much control role and procedure can preserve once pressure crosses a threshold.
public_vs_private.what_they_refuse_to_admit_to_themselves: That some of their identity is now fused to crisis-management rather than to the clean ideal of the role.

psychology.what_they_want_most: Useful clarity, respect for Recruiter, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
psychology.what_they_are_trying_to_protect_or_avoid: Keep Red Hood Guild workable through the obligations of recruiter while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
psychology.emotional_drive: Beneath the role sits a need for ordinary life under institutional pressure, legibility, and a world that does not collapse into preventable noise.
psychology.core_wound: No singular foundational trauma is directly asserted in canon; the usable wound layer is role-shaped wear, exposure, and the cost of being the person who must notice first or answer last.
psychology.how_it_shaped_them: It turned ordinary duty into a personality contour: they scan for failure early, trust slowly, and measure strangers by downstream cost.
psychology.guiding_belief: If the system is kept legible, losses can still be bounded.
psychology.hidden_belief: Someone always pays for confusion; better it be controlled sooner than wider later.
psychology.lie_they_tell_themselves: That they are only doing the job and that the job has not already begun shaping what they are.
psychology.truth_they_are_avoiding: Their daily choices influence culture, fear, and legitimacy far beyond the narrow wording of the role.

thresholds.anger: being forced to absorb avoidable disorder
thresholds.fear: cascading failure that makes role performance meaningless
thresholds.power: clear information, recognized standing, and procedural initiative
thresholds.smallness: being bypassed, misfiled, or rendered decorative

morality_in_practice.how_they_justify_actions: By reference to burden, continuity, and the need to keep the surrounding place from slipping into a harder consequence tier.
morality_in_practice.what_they_consider_unforgivable: Willful sabotage of trust, records, access, or public safety tied to their role.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_absolutely_do: Delay, question, redirect, document, and escalate when place logic demands it.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_only_do_under_pressure: Leverage reputation, force, secrecy, or rigid procedure in ways that damage future trust.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_never_do: Act against corpus-confirmed identity or role logic without a visible pressure bridge.
morality_in_practice.relationship_to_good_evil: Operational read: Complicated / Conflicted. Their danger lies less in theatrical evil than in what happens when role logic outruns compassion, nuance, or proportionality.

power_profile.why_they_matter: Sel Stone can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
power_profile.why_hard_to_ignore: By enforcing Recruiter too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
power_profile.secondary_source_of_power: local familiarity, institutional memory, and other people needing something from them
power_profile.why_difficult_to_confront_directly: Confronting them usually means confronting place rules, witnesses, and downstream consequences rather than only the individual body in front of you.

capabilities.magic: Not explicitly stated unless separately asserted elsewhere in corpus.
capabilities.combat: Role-dependent; implied only to the degree their office already carries enforcement, survival, or defensive expectation.
capabilities.social: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a recruiter still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
capabilities.strategic_administrative: Performs the ongoing duties of recruiter in and around Red Hood Guild, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
capabilities.logistical_economic: Keep Red Hood Guild workable through the obligations of recruiter while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
capabilities.investigative_covert: Must stay useful at the seam where policy becomes ordinary consequence. Around Red Hood Guild, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of recruiter visibly slip.

signature_methods.how_they_typically_win: By controlling tempo, framing the problem first, and forcing others to deal with the local reality they represent.
signature_methods.how_they_escalate: If crossed, Sel Stone adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
signature_methods.when_cornered: They narrow options, call on procedure or allies, and try to move the conflict onto terrain they understand better than the intruder.

systems_impact.cultural_effect: Ordinary people around Red Hood Guild learn to speak, wait, disclose, or hide differently depending on whether Sel Stone or people like them are part of the scene.

society_politics_geopolitics.ordinary_people_experience: Common folk experience Sel Stone as a point where broader pressures become personal: papers checked, routes delayed, rites clarified, access granted, warnings issued, or burdens redirected.
society_politics_geopolitics.cultural_wound_or_benefit: They leave behind either increased legibility and trust or accumulated caution and narrowed speech.
society_politics_geopolitics.secondary_behaviors: People rehearse their story before approaching; they gather proof early; they watch who is seen speaking with this NPC.
factions_fear: actors who benefit from vagueness, delay, leakage, or unmanaged pressure
society_politics_geopolitics.governance_shift: At table scale, this NPC shifts how authority feels inside Red Hood Guild: more navigable if cooperative, more costly if crossed.
society_politics_geopolitics.geopolitical_effect: Small incidents around Sel Stone can propagate upward through Transpolity if they touch routes, records, trade, ritual legitimacy, or public order.

relationships.note: Both tied to Red Hood Guild.

hooks.summary: Getting involved with Sel Stone can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Sel Stone can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Recruiter.
